name: 2017 EULAR/ACR Classification Criteria for Adult and Juvenile Idiopathic Inflammatory Myopathies
citation: Lundberg IE, et al. Ann Rheum Dis. 2017;76(12):1955-1964.
classification_rule: Probability-based score. Probability ≥55% = possible IIM; ≥60% definite with biopsy; ≥90% definite without biopsy.

items_without_biopsy.label: Age of onset of first symptom related to disease ≥18 and <40 yrs
Age of onset of first symptom ≥40 yrs
Objective symmetric weakness, proximal upper extremities
Objective symmetric weakness, proximal lower extremities
Neck flexors relatively weaker than neck extensors
In legs, proximal muscles relatively weaker than distal
Elevated serum CK or LDH or AST or ALT

items_with_biopsy_addon.label: Endomysial infiltrate without invading non-necrotic muscle fibres

notes_on_4_7_reasoning: For Asian male with multi-organ inflammation, normal CK but muscle soreness and interstitial lung findings, strongly weight anti-MDA5 dermatomyositis.
Anti-MDA5, anti-Jo-1, anti-PL-7/PL-12, anti-TIF1γ sit on the MYOP18 panel — pending results are rate-limiting.
TIF1γ positivity in adults ≥40 raises cancer screen urgency (paraneoplastic DM).

references.title: 2017 European League Against Rheumatism/American College of Rheumatology classification criteria for adult and juvenile idiopathic inflammatory myopathies and their major subgroups
references.authors: Lundberg IE, Tjärnlund A, Bottai M, et al.
references.why_it_matters: The current reference classification. Replaces Bohan and Peter 1975. Introduces probability-scoring and subgroup classification (PM, DM, amyopathic DM, juvenile DM, IBM). Published jointly in Ann Rheum Dis and Arthritis Rheumatol.

references.title: Inflammatory muscle diseases
references.why_it_matters: Definitive NEJM review of IIM by one of the field's architects. Covers PM, DM, necrotising autoimmune myositis, IBM, including histopathology, autoantibody classification, and treatment.

references.title: Autoantibodies in myositis
references.why_it_matters: Authoritative reference on myositis-specific (Jo-1, PL-7, PL-12, EJ, OJ, SRP, HMGCR, Mi-2, MDA5, TIF-1γ, NXP-2, SAE) and myositis-associated (Ro52, PM-Scl, U1-RNP, Ku) antibodies, their clinical phenotypes, and prognostic value.

references.title: RNA helicase encoded by melanoma differentiation-associated gene 5 is a major autoantigen in patients with clinically amyopathic dermatomyositis: association with rapidly progressive interstitial lung disease
references.authors: Sato S, Hoshino K, Satoh T, Fujita T, Kawakami Y, Fujita T, Kuwana M
references.why_it_matters: Originating paper identifying MDA5 (previously anti-CADM-140) as the autoantigen linked to rapidly progressive ILD in clinically amyopathic DM. Highly relevant for East Asian male patients with normal CK and interstitial lung findings.

references.title: Most patients with cancer-associated dermatomyositis have antibodies to nuclear matrix protein NXP-2 or transcription intermediary factor 1γ
references.authors: Fiorentino DF, Chung LS, Christopher-Stine L, et al.
references.why_it_matters: TIF-1γ and NXP-2 positivity in adults carries a strong association with occult malignancy (up to 75% of TIF-1γ+ adult DM patients). Triggers cancer screening protocol.

references.title: Inflammatory myopathies: Evaluation and management
references.authors: Selva-O'Callaghan A, Pinal-Fernandez I, Trallero-Araguás E, Milisenda JC, Grau-Junyent JM, Mammen AL
references.why_it_matters: Contemporary Lancet Neurology evaluation-and-management review covering diagnostic algorithms, muscle MRI, muscle biopsy indications, and tiered immunosuppression.

references.title: Idiopathic inflammatory myopathies
references.authors: Lundberg IE, Fujimoto M, Vencovsky J, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Comprehensive Nature Primer covering epidemiology, mechanism, diagnosis, management, quality of life, and outlook across the IIM spectrum.
